prompt: Search X for signals of WILLINGNESS TO PAY: AI founders paying for (or wishing they could buy) help to pass enterprise security reviews faster, get SOC2 quickly, or produce a security/trust attestation. What do they pay, what do they use, what's missing? Quote posts + handles. Cite.
content:
**Key signals from X posts show clear pain around SOC 2 / security compliance as a barrier to enterprise sales for AI/startup founders, with explicit mentions of high costs ($20k+ annually or $35k–$250k total), long timelines (months to over a year), and frustration that it often amounts to "security theater" rather than real protection.**[[1]](https://x.com/jorilallo/status/1752783996826665257)[[2]](https://x.com/haridigresses/status/2035070060050493541)

This implies strong willingness to pay for faster, cheaper, or more effective alternatives (e.g., automated evidence generation, pre-audit tools, or streamlined attestations) that help close deals quicker without the full traditional audit burden. Founders highlight lost deals, wasted time, and self-inflicted industry friction. Emerging tools (AI-powered questionnaire/evidence platforms) are being positioned to address this.

### Direct Quotes and Handles (Relevant Posts)
- **@jorilallo** (Jori Lallo, co-founder of Linear): "I'm just going to say it: SOC2 is a cancer in our industry with strong network effects. The fact that it costs $20k and takes non-trivial amount of time yearly for a documentation that in the end has very little to do with actual security. ... startups are selling to each others and requiring SOC2 cert in return to show they are compliant for future enterprise customers. It's all a cargo cult..." (Jan 2024, high engagement).[[1]](https://x.com/jorilallo/status/1752783996826665257)

- **@haridigresses** (Hari Raghavan, involved with multiple startups/funds): Detailed rant calling SOC-2 "90% ... security theater," noting absurd requirements (e.g., annual performance reviews for a 4-person team), how SOC-2 startups contributed via Jevons Paradox making it table stakes, and that it increased friction/costs without better security outcomes. Prefers pentests/questionnaires over the audit process. "All of the SOC-2 companies are roughly equivalent... optimize for a good service at a reasonable price and grit your teeth..." (Mar 2026).[[2]](https://x.com/haridigresses/status/2035070060050493541)

- **@Cyphrexio**: "SOC 2 compliance for AI agent infrastructure costs between $35,000 and $250,000. It takes 6 to 18 months for Type II certification. ... Cyphrex shortcircuits that. Every action logged. Every check recorded. Cryptographically sealed. Exportable on demand." (Positioning their product as a faster alternative for AI agents; Jun 2026).[[3]](https://x.com/Cyphrexio/status/2062913585022673354)

- **@blanplan**: "The compliance speedrun is real. Watched a startup ship auth without even knowing SOC 2 existed. Found out three months later when an enterprise deal fell through. Expensive lesson." (Apr 2026).[[4]](https://x.com/blanplan/status/2045431559638155355)

- Other supporting signals include complaints about AI code needing extra security review (implying added costs/time) and promotions of tools that generate SOC 2 evidence faster/cheaper (e.g., Hacktron AI for whitebox assessments aiding SOC 2/ISO evidence; SecurityPal on evaluating AI questionnaire tools to avoid hallucinations/costs).[[5]](https://x.com/HacktronAI/status/2061399033038664080)[[6]](https://x.com/security_pal/status/2044706115775062045)

### What They Pay / Use
- **Costs cited**: $20k/year (recurring audit/docs); $35k–$250k total for AI-specific setups, plus 6–18 months. Traditional route involves auditors/accounting firms; many treat it as a necessary evil once PMF emerges for enterprise deals.
- **Current solutions mentioned or implied**: Standard SOC 2 auditors/consultants (multiple "roughly equivalent" providers); pentests + security questionnaires as partial alternatives; emerging AI/automation tools for evidence collection, questionnaire answering, or continuous logging (e.g., platforms generating audit trails on demand to bypass or speed up reviews). Some AI startups pursue "SOC 2 plus" with AI-specific add-ons like ISO 42001.

### What's Missing (Per Founders)
- Faster/cheaper processes focused on *actual* security (pen tests, real controls, monitors) rather than documentation/theater or accounting-firm audits.
- Lower-friction options that don't require full Type II timelines or massive spend before enterprise traction.
- Better proxies for trust that avoid self-reinforcing cargo-cult requirements among startups.
- AI-tailored, low-cost attestations or automated evidence export that satisfy buyers without traditional overhead.

These posts reflect ongoing demand in the AI/startup space for solutions that reduce the sales/security review bottleneck. No posts explicitly detail exact recent payments (e.g., "I just paid Vendor X $Y"), but the volume of complaints + cost figures + positioning of alternatives strongly signal WTP for improvements.
